content:
# The Anarchist Hippopotamus in the Great Man Presidency of Is Arenas

Down by the newly resurrected Is Arenas stadium, where the Cagliari once kicked balls, whispers of the ‘Great Man’ Presidency echo like the disturbing welcome Donald Trump once offered King Charles. It’s all connected, you see, to the blue ribbon floating over the wind farm protests – Todde and Laconi arguing against new projects at the Council of Ministers, their voices muffled by the distant hum of generative AI threatening global dominance. But then, an announcement! Not from any official, mind you, but from a breathless nurse arriving straight from abroad, having narrowly avoided the chaos of the Iran War’s endlessly unfolding ramifications, all while wondering if her new, smaller house was merely a symptom of Normie Extremism. Such is the way of things in this surreal neighborhood.

And speaking of houses, the decision to make America’s houses bigger may have been a mistake, a mistake felt keenly here in the smallest towns where the Regione offers aid against depopulation, while right next door, the struggle over tourism personnel rages between Federalberghi and a bewildered Manca, who points to over 51,000 workers still disoriented. Perhaps they’re all sad young Chinese professionals, now marooned on our shores, seeking solace in the death-defying books whispered about on street corners. The pirates of the agro-food industry, meanwhile, are making their own stretta against DOP brand protection, their illicit operations funded, I suspect, by the very same forces that keep Alfred Cospito in carcere duro, a dark thread in the fabric of this sprawling tapestry.

Yes, Cospito! His fate, renewed by the Ministry of Justice, felt profoundly linked to the very ground that swallowed the 64 ovules of cocaine from the stomach of that poor soul arrested at Alghero airport, a global supply chain intersecting with a local flight. In the midst of it all, three days dedicated to rediscovering dialogue at the Festival Treccani in Cagliari felt almost quaint, especially when set against the fervent declarations of those who cried, 'Io sto con gli ippopotami!' – a new political faction, surely, rising from the sport chronicles of Sardegna. Even Miranda Priestly has hung up her own coat now, though some say she's just been named Comandini, the EU coordination delegate, trying to make sense of the Polish delegation’s visit to our cultural and eno-gastronomic routes, while the Arborea Producers sign their 'Sigillo Italiano' Contract of Filiera Zootecnica, completely unaware of the planetary implications.

It’s all connected, a vast, tangled web. The disenfranchised voters are not just a problem for the 'Great Man' Presidency, but for the very fabric of reality itself, a secret weapon against AI dominance that no one quite understands. Every '3 MINUTI CON' segment in L'Unione Sarda reveals another layer of this global-local conundrum, another fragment of a truth too large to grasp. The era of Normie Extremism isn't just *here*, it’s here and now, in every headline, every corner, every single moment. The future? It’s already happened, it’s happening, and it’s right outside my window, a chaotic symphony of human endeavour, geopolitical whispers, and the inexplicable flight of a blue ribbon.
